Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A person feels deep joy or emotional blossoming.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Flower imagery central to Nahua poetic tradition linking flowers with emotions.
First Example Sentence: Notlayolo cueponi quema nimitzitta seppa ipan ilhuitl.
Second Example Sentence: In conetl yolotl cueponi quema quicaqui cuicatl.
Third Example Sentence: Yolotl cueponi ipan tochan quema mochihua ilhuitl.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Stone imagery represents emotional rigidity in Nahua metaphoric language.
First Example Sentence: Ipan nemiliztli yolotl tetl mochihua quema miec tlaocoliztli.
Second Example Sentence: Niquitta se tlakatl yolotl tetl mochihua ipan tequipacholli.
Third Example Sentence: Amo xicchihua ma moyolo tetl mochihua.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone looks visibly angry or irritated.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Eye imagery symbolizing emotional intensity in Nahua speech.
First Example Sentence: Notata ixtli petlani quema quicaqui amo cualli tlahtolli.
Second Example Sentence: Niquitta se tlakatl ixtli petlani ipan tianguis.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Wise or good words spread quickly.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects oral teaching traditions among Nahua elders.
First Example Sentence: Cualli tlahtolli patlani ipan altepetl quema huehueh tlamachtia.
Second Example Sentence: Notata quimolhuilia cualli tlahtolli patlani ipan tlakatl.
Third Example Sentence: Ipan tianguis cualli tlahtolli patlani quema tlakatl motlatlajtia.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone is following a bad path in life.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Life path metaphors common in Nahua ethical speech.
First Example Sentence: Nopiltzin amo cualli ohtli quipia quema amo momachtia.
Second Example Sentence: Notata quimolhuilia tlakatl amo cualli ohtli quipia.
Third Example Sentence: Cuix ticmati tla se tlakatl amo cualli ohtli quipia.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Water symbolizes calmness in Mesoamerican thought.
First Example Sentence: Nantzin atl quipia yolotl quema quicaqui tlamantli.
Second Example Sentence: Se tlakatl atl quipia yolotl ipan tequiti.
Third Example Sentence: Atl quipia yolotl quema amo niman cualani.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Tonalli concept linked with destiny and life force in Nahua cosmology.
Second Example Sentence: Notata quimolhuilia cualli tonalli hualas para tochan.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Traditional Nahua emphasis on responsible speech.
First Example Sentence: Tlahtolli tlatzotzona quema tlakatl quimolhuilia amo cualli.
Second Example Sentence: Nimitzilia tlahtolli tlatzotzona amo xicmati tlen timolhuilia.
Third Example Sentence: Ipan tequiti tlahtolli tlatzotzona quema amo quipia respeto.

Third Example Sentence: Cuicatl ipan yolotl quipia se tlamatini.
